what_it_does: This file is a research-synthesis document that consolidates two rounds of investigation into existing codebase reuse and broader architectural fit for a concurrent-cognition build, producing a one-line ledger of reusable versus net-new components, hardware facts, and open developer decisions.
observations: It is the third document of a triad (Criteria + Guide + Synthesis) and is hardened by `review/R1-FOLD.md`.
Round 1 maps existing code artifacts to criteria G1–G8 with file:line evidence and a reuse spine.
Round 2 surveys external architectures, node-mechanism build-out, applications, a model+capability registry, and SDD coordination.
Explicit review corrections move injection, `chat_parts`, the G5 activation substrate, and the jury flow from reuse to net-new.
A resource correction states that the resident 4B uses `max_num_seqs=16` with a KV pool shared with the main context, so real co-resident concurrency is well below 32 at usable context.
It provides a one-line ledger separating near-wholesale reuse from net-new build items.
It lists verified hardware facts and leaves several developer decisions open, referencing `DECISIONS.md`.
It relies heavily on criterion IDs (e.g., G1.1, G2) and file:line references without inline definitions.
standouts: The central correction that assumed reuse shrank and several key pieces are net-new.
Concurrency width is state-dependent and currently capped by shared KV pool / `max_num_seqs=16`, not a flat 32.
The design is positioned as a literature-gap fusion: parallel-for-quality + one-model-many-roles + interact-during-streamed-reply.
The proposed build method is one serial spine plus disjoint lanes, with recursive coordination deferred until after G1 exists.
conventions: Markdown header hierarchy with sections and bullet lists.
Cross-references via criterion IDs (G1.1, G2, etc.).
Review-driven correction notes placed at the top and inline.
Structured as explored / verified findings / implications for the build.
concerns: Nested corrections and bracketed errata make the concurrency figure hard to read and may indicate an unresolved number.
Heavy reliance on external criterion IDs and file:line references makes the file opaque without the rest of the triad.
Open dev-calls are surfaced but not resolved here.
Evidence-base statements and design-positioning claims sit side by side without clear separation.
notes: A build-prep research synthesis that reclassifies assumed reuse as net-new and anchors the concurrent-cognition plan on state-dependent concurrency limits and a one-line reuse ledger.
questions: What do the criterion IDs (G1.1, G2, etc.) map to without the Criteria document?
Which specific code changes turn each net-new item into implementation?
What is the final agreed concurrency width under the live voice config?
How are the open dev-calls in `DECISIONS.md` resolved and by whom?
What is the precise content of `R1-FOLD.md` and `R2-FOLD.md` that is treated as binding?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to consolidate research waves into an evidence base; the file does that, while also functioning as a corrected planning ledger and build-scoping document.
intention_signals: It tries to ground an ambitious concurrent-cognition architecture in existing code reality, correct over-optimistic reuse assumptions, and produce a shared reference that lets the team scope build work before coding.
novelty: The explicit reuse-shrank correction and the one-line ledger; positioning the design as a literature-gap fusion rather than a named existing pattern.
contribution: A corrected inventory of which existing components can be reused and which must be built, plus state-dependent concurrency facts and a method for serial-spine-plus-lane build coordination.
summary_for_embedding: Research synthesis document consolidating codebase-reuse and broader-architecture findings for a concurrent-cognition build, listing reusable components, net-new build items, hardware limits, and open decisions.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the evidence base and corrected scoping ledger supporting the Criteria and Guide before spike-gated implementation.
